factors.summary: Cintas has seen improvements in productivity through initiatives like optimizing delivery routes and sourcing.

evidence.text: The increase was driven primarily by improved sourcing, leveraging of existing warehouses and optimization of delivery routes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2017

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a significant increase in cash flows from operating activities.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $660.9 million... an increase of $177.1 million compared to the nine months ended February 28, 2017.

factors.summary: The company has successfully deleveraged through paying off term loans and managing its commercial paper and long-term debt effectively.

evidence.text: Cintas paid off the term loan balance of $250.0 million with cash on hand. On December 1, 2017, Cintas paid the $300.0 million aggregate principal amount of its 6.13% 10-year senior notes... with cash on hand and $265.0 million in proceeds from the issuance of commercial paper.

factors.summary: The company has a share buyback program in place, with no buybacks under the latest program as of the reporting date.

evidence.text: On August 2, 2016, we announced that the Board of Directors authorized a new $500.0 million share buyback program... As of February 28, 2018, no share buybacks have occurred under the August 2, 2016 program...

factors.summary: The company faces routine legal proceedings, but management believes the aggregate liability is not material.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to legal proceedings... the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect...

factors.summary: The company is experiencing consistent organic revenue growth, primarily driven by increased sales volume. The growth rates vary slightly between the three-month and nine-month periods, with organic revenue increasing by 7.8% and 10.9% respectively.

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 7.8% as a result of increased sales volume. Organic growth adjusts for the impact of acquisitions and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2017

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 10.9% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2017

factors.summary: The company faced short-term margin pressures due to integration expenses from the G&K acquisition but demonstrated cost management capabilities through improved sourcing, leveraging existing warehouses, and optimizing delivery routes.

evidence.text: Cintas incurred integration expenses related to the G&K acquisition, impacting margins in the short term.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2017

evidence.text: Operating income for the three months ended February 28, 2018 was negatively impacted by $9.8 million of integration expenses incurred in connection with the G&K acquisition.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2017

evidence.text: The increase [in gross margin] was driven primarily by improved sourcing, leveraging of existing warehouses and optimization of delivery routes.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2017

factors.summary: The company experienced a mixed margin trajectory in the twelve-month period. While the gross margins decreased in the first three months due to the G&K acquisition with lower margins, there was an improvement in both gross and operating margins over the nine-month period.

evidence.text: The gross margin was 100 basis points lower than the prior fiscal year’s third quarter gross margin of 45.1% . The decrease was driven by the recent G&K acquisition, which had lower margins than our legacy Cintas margins.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2017

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 47.1%... an increase of 150 basis points... Selling and administrative expenses as a percent of revenue improved to 34.1%... decrease in selling and administrative expenses as a percent to revenue.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2017

factors.summary: Cintas has been actively engaged in acquisition activities, particularly within the Uniform Rental and Facility Services segment, which has contributed positively to the company's growth. The completion of the significant acquisition of G&K Services, Inc., valued at approximately $2.1 billion, has had a positive impact on revenue growth. Additionally, the company has made further investments through cash payments of $12.3 million for other business acquisitions, further supporting its expansion efforts in these segments.

evidence.text: On March 21, 2017, Cintas completed the acquisition of G&K Services, Inc. (G&K) for consideration of approximately $2.1 billion.
Cash paid for acquisitions of businesses was $12.3 million... primarily relate to expansion efforts in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment.
